channel.title: Announcements (new threads)
channel.description: Threads in the forum category "Announcements" - Announce new pages and site-wide events here. Authors, please use the collective SCP, Tale, GOI Entry, and Update threads for new works.

item.title: SCP Announcements 47: Life is like riding a bicycle.
item.description: ANNOUNCE NEW SCPS HERE! Please do not post sandbox links. Those go in the Drafts forum.
item.content:encoded:
To keep your balance you must keep moving.

TO ANNOUNCE AN SCP, PLEASE SCROLL DOWN AND CLICK "NEW POST".

Please do not post sandbox drafts here. Those should be brought to the Drafts Critique forum and/or the IRC chatrooms for feedback.

item.title: Underread and Underrated 2020 Peoples' Choice
item.description: One of your favorites not get the respect it deserved?
item.content:encoded:
Hello!

For next year's1 Underread and Underrated, I've decided to source recommendations for the most underrated pieces of 2020. To this end, I've made a survey, which will remain open until January 15th, 2021.

Here's a link to the survey. Have fun and let me know what y'all think!

Footnotes
1. February

item.title: Deletions 70: If the fact will not fit the theory – let the theory go.
item.description: This is the current deletions thread.
item.content:encoded:
All Staff-enacted Deletions are carried out according to the Deletions Guide.

Summary deletions result for:

  • Articles with malicious intent
  • Pages that are clearly unfinished.
  • Attempts to "game" site procedure, such as re-posting a non-rewritten article in an attempt to get around downvotes.
  • Blatantly plagiarized material.
  • An article posted outside of the current range.

If an author attempts to self-delete their work and fails to do so properly, including either by blanking the page or renaming it, it will be deleted properly by Staff.

item.title: 9th Annual Holiday Art Exchange~!
item.description: "No, no, you don't get it at all. I mean 'Holiday Art Exchange.' You know, Secret Santa and ho-ho-ho and presents to pretty people?"
item.content:encoded:
It's November 15th. The world is getting colder. Snow is beginning to grace the ground. Thanksgiving is soon. Christmas, Hanukkah, Kwanzaa, Yule, and a whole assortment of other holidays are soon to follow. But first, we have our own, special, SCP only holiday to celebrate. It's actually a whole festival, that starts today, and ends on Dec. 29th. It's called…

The SCP Holiday Art Exchange~!

(hosted by DarkStuff with his lovely assistant, Elogee FishTruck)


The Overview

Hold up, what is this?

Glad you asked! This is an annual event on the SCP wiki in which people exchange gifts, Secret Santa style! The giftee doesn't know who their gifter is until several days after receiving the gift itself, and everyone gets to have a ton of fun making other people happy and being made happy.


Hmm, what kind of gifts can be exchanged?

Gifts can be anything that can be given over the internet, but, due to the community, are most often writing, visual art, or some other kind of media that can be given with a link! Though optional, plenty of the gifts exchanged during the art exchange are later posted as tales or SCPs on the site, or posted onto the respective art pages of the gifting artists.


Y'know, that's pretty neat, I'm in! How do I join?

Glad to have you on board! You can participate by responding to this thread with this format:

What I Can Give:

What I Am Looking For:

And of course fill in after the colons with the respective information. Before you do, however, let me impart onto you some wisdom that has been learned from previous years… don't, I repeat, don't put that you'd be "alright with anything" in the What I Am Looking For section! You may think that you are being considerate and making the process easier, but this, in fact, is one of the hardest wishes to fulfill, because your giftee will be directionless! Please make it something more specific than that. And speaking of rules…

Don't sign up if you are iffy on being able to make the deadlines, laid out below!

This art exchange runs on a schedule. You will be expected to have a gift ready to turn in by the deadline. If you're unsure you can make that, it'd be best to abstain for this year. That said, if something unexpected comes up in the over-a-month between now and then, we get it and will not blame you. If such a thing were to occur, you have two options:

  1. Send in an unfinished gift at the deadline with the promise of finishing it later, or…
  2. Contact us! Send either me or Elogee FishTruck a PM as soon as possible, and we'll get a pinch-hitter on it right away, no harm no foul. More on that later!


The Schedule

First, a brief overview.

Nov. 15th - Nov. 28th — Gathering participants.
Nov. 30th - Dec. 22nd — Making the gifts.
Dec. 23rd - Dec. 24th — Pinch-hitting.
Dec. 25th - Dec. 28th — Receive gifts, guess at giftees.
Dec. 29th — Revealing the Secret Santas.
Anytime after Dec. 29th — Optional posting of gifts to the wiki.

Now, something more in-depth:

How It's All Gonna Go
& When

Nov. 15th - Nov. 28th — Gathering Participants

This is the period during which you can reply to this very thread with the "what I can give, what I would like" format. After this period is over, no one more can join, so you're going to have to profess your participation before Nov. 28th, at 21:00 PST!


Nov. 30th - Dec. 22nd — Making the Gifts

Between Nov. 28th and Nov. 30th, I (and perhaps Elogee FishTruck) will be using black magic to determine who will be Secret Santa for who, and on Nov. 30th, we will release a barrage of Wikidot PMs which will secretly enlighten you as to who are making gifts for! When these get sent out will be variable, but look for them on the 30th. As soon as you have your PM, and have been enlightened as to what your giftee wants, it is up to you to create your gift for them!

You may turn in your gift any time between Nov. 30th and Dec. 22nd, but the hard deadline is Dec. 22nd at 21:00 PST. By the way, you may be wondering. How can you keep your writing gift anonymous? Well, you can use Pastebin as a text dump just like people can use Imgur as an image dump. But, if you'd like it to visibly show Wikidot formatting, you can copy the following CSS into a sandbox page:

[[module css]]
a#history-button, a#files-button {
display: none;
}
[[/module]]

This will make it so that the History button at the bottom of the page doesn't show up, keeping your writing completely anonymous~!

How do you turn your gift in? You can turn in your gift by Wikidot PMing either myself or Elogee FishTruck with the gift in question. Please, make it easy on us by saying "my gift for [person]", so we don't have to search through our own spreadsheet to find out who you made a gift for. And as a reminder, do not ghost the Art Exchange! If you suddenly realize you can't make the deadline, tell us. There is no shame in realizing it isn't going to work out. There is a little bit in dropping off the face of the earth with no warning. So please just say!


Dec. 23rd - Dec. 24th — Pinch-Hitting

During this time, I and others (the pinch-hitters) will work to fill in for any gifters that didn't make the deadline. It's hectic, we may end up with works in progress instead of finished products, but it will happen, and we will work together to ensure the most holiday joy possible.


Dec. 25th - Dec. 28th — Receive Gifts, Guess at Giftees

At some time during Christmas day, Dec. 25th, every lovely participant will receive their gift anonymously via a PM from myself or Elogee FishTruck. We encourage you to post here, on this thread, with a link to your gift, and your own thoughts on it! Then, people can experience everyone's gifts together, and we can revel in guessing at who made what!


Dec. 29th — Revealing Secret Santas

On Dec. 29th, at 10:00 PST, I will reveal who made what gifts with a post here on this thread. Then everyone can talk to their giftees, discuss the aftermath, celebrate, what have you, et cetera and onward! It's a great big occasion and it's tons of fun to see if your guess was correct, or if you were completely off the mark. Plus, you can then talk to your giftees about perhaps some revisions before it goes up on the site, if going up on the site is a plan. Speaking of…


Anytime after Dec. 29th — Optional Posting of Gifts to the Wiki

If your gift that you gave fits on the wiki, as a tale or SCP or GoI format, then I encourage you to post when you are ready! Remember to add the tag "art-exchange", and then link to your piece in the Art Exchange Hub, under the 2020 section! Oh, and we'll be doing something new this year…

The second most common type of gift to give and receive in this art exchange is visual art, so we're going to add a section to the hub for all the visual art that gets shared here! We're not exactly sure of the format yet, but be on the lookout for that, because we will know what we're doing and how you can add your art to it by Dec. 29th, and will edit those instructions into this here post. Wowza!


Pinch-Hitting & Friendly Reminders

About being a pinch-hitter.

Pinch-hitters are the people who have from Dec. 23rd to Dec. 24th to create a gift for giftees who have absentee gifters. You may have more than this amount of time if a gifter reveals they won't be able to make it some time before the deadline, but worst case (and most likely) scenario, you will have 48 hours to create (or make headway into) a gift, which means…

Don't become a pinch-hitter unless you are very confident in your ability! On top of that, I may refuse you as a pinch-hitter if I don't think you can do it. Harsh, I know, but the only way I can be confident in you is if you're known and I know you (or you can be vouched for). It's all in the aid of getting everyone gifts they can be happy with, on-time. If you would like to be a pinch-hitter, simply say so at the bottom of your post you make on this thread.


Final, friendly reminders!

  • Don't ghost the exchange. Please, if you can't make it, send a PM beforehand.
  • It's called Secret Santa! Don't reveal yourself to your giftee, and if you know something, don't let anyone in on it! Secrecy is part of the fun!
  • Keep it jolly and festive! Don't intentionally make a gift that goes against the giftee's wishes, or otherwise knowingly make something bad.
  • Oh, and no smut please. The content on this site can definitely end up in the NSFW zone, but very few people are here for erotica, and last year there was an issue with that, so we're making it a rule!

Alright, with all of that out of the way…

Let the Merriment Begin~!

item.title: We are Moving IRC Networks starting Today!
item.content:encoded:
In what was the worst-kept secret of September, we are ready to begin migrating away from our current IRC chat network and on to a new self-hosted one. Our current network is badly outdated with no real plan to address that, and has grown more unreliable and with less administrative support over the last couple of years.

Starting tonight, we will be moving the official channels of #site19, #site17, and #thecritters as well as the Secretary_Helen bot to irc.scpwiki.com. You can access it via ports 6660-6669, 6697 with SSL and SASL enabled, or via the web client here. An update to the Chat Guide will be posted when we begin the migration in earnest.

If you use IRCCloud, you will notice a ton of new features like typing indicators, reactions, threaded replies, and the ability to edit and delete posts.

I will be available on both networks for questions as well as here. For help specifically on the new network please connect either to #help or #site17 on SkipIRC.

item.title: Think you could host a seminar? Apply here to host!
item.description: Apply here to be considered by the Seminars team to host a seminar for the community!
item.content:encoded:
The Seminars Team is looking for experienced and skilled writers on the site who feel confident in any subject of their choosing, and want to share that knowledge with the community! Whether it’s specific types of dialogue, action sequences, plot lines, suspense, or anything else - we're interested in a wide variety of subjects and topics from experienced users.

To apply to host a seminar, please provide us with the following in a reply to this post:

1. The idea/topic for the seminar you’d like to host (examples include action sequences, argument dialogue, suspense building, et cetera. Don’t be afraid of suggesting anything that you think might be a little far-fetched, we’re always looking for new things!)
2. At least 3 examples of works you have posted to this wiki that show your skills in the above
3. Best way to contact you (Wikidot/IRC/Discord)

Once you’ve applied, it may take a few days for a decision to be made. If your application is accepted, a member of the Seminars team will reach out to you. Note that this thread is not intended for junior staff applications. Those can be found here.

Perks of hosting a seminar:

1. A social media post advertising an article of your choice
2. A mention of your seminar and an article of your choosing in the Site News for that month
3. Advertise your work - you can use it as examples in your seminar!
4. Help the community learn and expand their writing skills
5. Get to know new people in the community

If you have any questions, contact taylor_itkin or TheMightyMcB.

item.title: Domain Transition Announcement
item.content:encoded:
Today, we're going to be transitioning our domain from scp-wiki.net to scpwiki.com. This is being done for a few reasons:

  • By having the simplest url (no dashes and on the more common .com instead of .net) it's easier to find the website which helps accessibility.
  • Our current domain is owned by a retired staffer who doesn't come around much these days, so this switch ensures that if any issues occur with that domain it won't prevent people from using the wiki. As is, if an issue occurred it could take days or weeks to fix depending on how long it takes to contact the owner.
  • It is more accurate. While the SCP wiki itself isn't a commercial endeavor, Wikidot still runs ads and collects revenue for itself on our traffic. It should be noted that both scp-wiki.net and scpwiki.com are custom urls that simply replace the actual url of the website, which is and always has been scp-wiki.wikidot.com.

The old url will still redirect seamlessly to the new one, so old links should still work. Enjoy using scpwiki.com!

item.title: Tale Announcements 18: Parsley, Sage, Rosemary, and Thyme
item.description: ANNOUNCE NEW TALES HERE! :D Please do not post sandbox links. Those go in the Drafts forum.
item.content:encoded:
TO ANNOUNCE A TALE, PLEASE SCROLL DOWN AND CLICK "NEW POST".

In-progress works go in a sandbox, and should be reviewed in the Drafts Critique forums prior to a mainsite post. Please do not announce sandbox drafts!

item.title: Community Outreach SEMINARS TEAM Junior Staff Recruitment - CLOSED
item.description: The heads of this team are taylor_itkin and TheMightyMcB. This team operates as a sub team underneath Community Outreach, which is ran by ProcyonLotor and A Random Day.
item.content:encoded:
The Seminars Team is currently searching for new Junior Staff members.

Primary Responsibilities:

  • Organizing and/or hosting seminars and workshops
  • Moderating seminars and workshops (including orientations)
  • Revising lesson plans
  • Providing input and new perspectives for developing seminars
  • Advertising seminars and workshops

Secondary Responsibilities:

  • Taking notes on seminars as they occur
  • Condensing seminar notes for public consumption
  • Keeping Seminars Team resources (hub, official sandbox, forum threads, etc) up to date

Competitive Perks:

  • An experience of staff ongoings, including policy discussions and other shenanigans
  • The ability to post to the staff site. You won't be responsible for voting or anything but can give your view on policies relevant to Community Outreach.
  • After ~4 months on the team, we are able to nominate you for promotion to fully Operational Staff (which occurs every 4 months). (Note: If we choose not to nominate you, and you're curious why, we will try to be completely open with you about it.)
  • No pressure- if it's not for you, there's nothing forcing you to keep at it.

PLEASE NOTE: The Seminars Team is still new, rapidly growing, and responsibilities and the needs of the team are still changing and expanding. Joining this team allows for the opportunity for you to not only gain responsibility but present new ideas to the team. Being on the Seminars team might require you to interact with high-level staff in a one-on-one setting. Please be aware of this.

If you're interested, please REPLY TO THIS POST with answers to the following questions:

Why do you want to be on JS for Community Outreach (specifically Seminars Team) and why are you a good fit?

How active are you, both on the site and on the chat?

How can we contact you (IRC/WikiDot/Discord)?

item.title: New Ideas Critique Forum Policy
item.content:encoded:
Noting staff discussion threads here: http://05command.wikidot.com/forum/t-13274176/forum-critique-policy-revision and here: http://05command.wikidot.com/forum/t-13268033/required-forum-activity

Per recent discussion, the current forum critique policy has changed for the Ideas Critique forum. Specifically, staff will not be immediately posting to all unanswered threads; rather, authors are expected to take the initiative to reach out to reviewers to seek feedback.

This change endeavors to direct reviewer attention to active, engaged authors who are familiar with site guidelines, thereby preventing an overabundance of time and energy devoted to threads that are never responded to or go inactive extremely quickly.

A new stickied thread listing various ways to seek feedback has been posted for authors new to the forums: http://www.scp-wiki.net/forum/t-13282692/how-to-get-more-reviews If there are any additions to be made to this list, feel free to comment here or PM me (Zyn) with recommendations.

item.title: 2019 SCP Community Survey
item.description: Make your voice heard! Complete the survey and give staff feedback on your site experience so we can continue making it better!
item.content:encoded: I'm pleased to announce the 2019 SCP Community Survey! Fill out this Google survey and make your voice heard! Tell us what we're doing well, what we're doing poorly, and what you'd like to see from staff and the site going forward. And feel free to check out survey results from previous years!

item.title: Wiki Orientations!
item.description: New users, join us for an introduction to the wiki and its culture!
item.content:encoded:
For any new users of the wiki who are interested:

The SCP Wiki Seminars Team will be hosting Orientation Seminars three times a year, with the next one being held on Saturday, March 14 at 2 pm EST. These seminars are designed to orient new users of the site so that they may better interact with the SCP community right from the get-go! It will cover topics like basic rules on site and in chat, basics about the SCP Universe, forum use, the posting and deletion process, and more!

These orientations will be an open forum for you to ask questions to all levels of staff and get a firm grasp on the culture of the wiki.

If you are interested in attending, all you need to do is hop onto the SynIRC network and join #workshop. More information on how to do this can be found on the wiki's Chat Guide.

For more information, please consult the Seminar and Workshops Hub or contact taylor_itkin or TheMightyMcB. If you cannot attend the seminar, an appended summary will be posted on the Seminar Notes Hub after the seminar.

item.title: Junior Staff Spotlight - The Good Noodles Awards! - Feb 2019 ver
item.description: A (hopefully) monthly initiative to highlight junior staff.
item.content:encoded:
Hi! This is the junior staff spotlight. It's been an ongoing initiative on and off for the past several months over among staff and we figured the rest of the site should partake in the good news. This thread is a shoutout to all the junior staff for the month of February. Round of applause for all of them.

cybersqyd has been absolutely phenomenal, replying to an incredible number of PM requests covering both the Ideas and Draft Critique forums. They've been kind and patient with every author they've encountered, and take care to follow up with discussions as authors revise their work. An indispensable team member as part of Forum crit!

gee0765 has been active in the chat and on the Ideas Critique forum, jumping in whenever we start to see some backlog and taking the initiative to address authors who aren't familiar with the forum guidelines. They have also been incredibly active in evaluating and critting 5kcon contestants as part of Site Crit work.

N_Aepic_Fael continuously drops placeholders on idea threads that happen to have fallen to the backlog, regardless of the kind of idea. He's a great inspiration for up-and-coming reviewers who are learning how to expand their expertise with regards to critiquing different kinds of material beyond the usual repetitive formulas we tend to see a lot. They also have been above and beyond in image formatting as part of the Licensing team!

Laneous for all of their work in evaluating and criting 5kcon contestants as part of Site Crit work.

aismallard for managing the walking for all of 5k (also shout-out to DrBleep!) for the Tech Team and the evaluation and critting of 5k contestants as part of Site Crit.

DrAkimoto for seminars work and Instagram revitalization in the Internet Outreach team.

Raddagher, MalyceGraves for their work in Seminars.

Cerastes for Instagram revitalization as part of the Internet Outreach team.

Yossipossi for their RU Fundraiser Graphics as part of the Internet Outreach team.

Deadly Bread and JackalRelated for their work in tabulating all collab pages as part of the Rewrite team.

Interested in joining in? Feel free to reach out!

Congratulations again on all the junior staff highlighted this month! Well done! There are not enough words of gratitude for all you do.

item.title: Donation Link For Licensing Emergency
item.content:encoded:
SCP Community,
Update: The Donation link is live! Please share, and donate at https://www.gofundme.com/f/scp-legal-funds

6 months ago, we alerted you to the actions of Andrey Duksin, a Russian man who has illegally registered an illegitimate trademark for SCP within the Eurasian Customs Union. He has used said trademark to threaten and extort legitimate sellers of SCP merchandise, and in addition is guilty of copyright infringement, as his own merchandise completely violates the SCP content license: Creative Commons Share-alike 3.0. For a time, the situation calmed as we slowly pursued the dissolution of Duksin’s illegitimate trademark via Rospatent, but it has now escalated.

Duksin has recently resumed his efforts to threaten and extort competitors, and has now begun to threaten SCP itself. He utilized the illegitimate trademark to shut down the official social media page of the Russian branch of the SCP Foundation Wiki, as well as a separate fan-page. We attempted to negotiate with VK, the social media company in question, but so long as the trademark registration stands they will abide by it. Now, Duksin has followed this by making a ridiculous demand to be administrator of the Russian wiki, and that said wiki be twisted into an advertisement for his merchandise rather than the writing community that it is.

These actions threaten not only the Russian community, but every SCP branch, writer, and fan around the world. We stand with SCP-RU, reject these threats, and are organizing a lawsuit against Duksin to annul his false trademark and prevent his continued copyright infringement. As an organization of volunteers, this is a measure we do not often pursue due to the costs involved.

Last May, we received many offers from generous SCP fans offering to donate to a legal fund. At the time we did not accept any offers, as we believed the situation could be resolved via diplomacy. With these new developments, this is no longer possible. As such, we humbly ask that anyone who loves SCP and has some money to spare donate to our legal fund here in order to protect our global community. The goal amount was determined via the advice of our legal counsel based on the costs of similar cases in the past. In the event that any money raised in this fundraiser remains following the conclusion of the lawsuit and any associated appeals, it will be held in an account specifically earmarked for the collective legal defense of the SCP Foundation branches.

The SCP community maintains a unified front against Duksin's threats. Please spread the word about this situation on social media using the hashtag #standwithscpru. With your help, SCP will continue to thrive.

item.title: 8th Annual Holiday Art Exchange
item.description: That's right, people, it's holiday time!
item.content:encoded:
Well gee, it’s that time of year again. The days are short, the music is jolly, and a general sense of wholesomeness fills one’s lungs. Time has flown by like snow from a furious blizzard. But speaking of furious blizzards and the outside damage they cause, it’s time to snuggle up in a warm cabin, drink some hot chocolate, and make things for other people over the internet. That’s right, it’s time for…

The 8th Annual SCP Holiday Art Exchange!!!

(Many thanks to DarkStuff who ran this last year)

General Information

Wait, what is this?

A yearly secret-Santa program where SCP wiki members ask for gifts, other SCP wiki members provide said gifts, and all the while the Giftee doesn’t know who their gifter is (even when receiving the gift)! It’s a chance for authors, artists, musicians, and whoever else alike to express their talents with surprise for both the recipient and the giver. It’s anonymous, fun, and festive!

Well, what exactly can be exchanged?

The sky’s the limit, really, but you must be able to answer yes to two very important questions regarding your gift before you send it.

“Did I make this?”
&
“Can I send this solely over the internet?” (No shipping things via plane, train, or automobile)

The gifts that people have sent to each other in the past range from tales to skips to art to music to readings to Wikidot avatars and games! Also, unofficial rule, have fun while making it.

I’m sold, how do I start?

Make a new post on this thread following this exact format:

 **What I Can Give:** What you can give **What I Am Looking For:** What you are looking for

Please make what you want at least somewhat specific. Saying “a tale about [insert subject here]” or something along those lines is fine, but saying you’ll take “anything” is simply too vague and is actually more stressing than if you were uber-specific. Also:

Don't sign up if you are iffy on being able to make the deadlines, laid out below!

That being said, if something unplanned comes up in your personal life and you can’t make a deadline, we can’t blame you for that and we’ll understand. There are two contingency plans for not finishing a gift on time. You can A) Send a WiP to your giftee and promise to finish it later, or B) Contact us and we’ll get a pinch-hitter on the case. More on that in a bit.

Schedule

+ Show Schedule
- Close

Stage 1: Signing up (Nov. 15th - Nov. 28th)

From the time this is posted to November 28th, 8 AM EST, simply follow the instructions for joining as spelled out above. When this stage is done, I'll post on this thread with a notification that no one else can sign up.

Stage 2: Names are assigned, work is conducted (Nov. 30th - Dec. 22nd)

Between Stages 1 and 2, I will use the ancient, arcane power of EXCEL SPREADSHEETS to make sure everyone is assigned to someone else. On the 30th, you will receive a PM from me assigning that someone to you with a link to their post on this thread.

You shall read your giftee's post and, from then until the 22nd of December at the latest, work on a project that fits the parameters of said post.

Remember that this is all anonymous. Therefore, don't tell your or anyone else's giftee their gifter, and work on your project in a way where your giftee won't know you're making it. The most common method if you're writing something is to use an anonymous Pastebin (if you're using this method, make sure, if you have one, you aren't logged onto your Pastebin account). There's also nifty CSS that can remove the history button from a Wikidot page, so if you sent someone a sandbox, they wouldn't be able to tell who made it. If that's easier for you, use this code:

 [[module css]] a#history-button, a#files-button { display: none; } [[/module]]

To turn a project in, send a Wikidot PM to me containing a link to the gift you made, with a title such as "Art Exchange Gift for [insert giftee's name here]" to make it easier on me. You may turn your gift in at any point between your giftee being assigned and December 22nd, 8 AM EST.

As mentioned earlier, if you cannot turn in your gift in the allotted timeframe, tell me so I can get something arranged early. Please do not ghost the Art Exchange. It's more stress for everyone involved if you do that, and it's a certified Scrooge Move™. Seriously, don't do it.

Stage 2.5: Last Minute Ensuring of Holiday Joy (Dec. 22nd - Dec. 24th)

Because it's almost guaranteed that someone will need to be fulfilled for without preliminary warning of the gifter being unavailable, this is the time where a pinch-hitter will work on your gift if your gifter has dropped off the face of the earth. While the loose deadline is the end of the 24th, a gift may require more time, and I'll alert you if that's the case.

I stress this once more, tell me if you cannot make the deadline beforehand. Try your absolute best to not, just, ghost the Art Exchange. It's a disservice to me, the pinch-hitters, and your giftee.

Stage 3: Receiving the Gifts, Wondering Who Made it (Dec. 25th - Dec. 28th)

Sometime on the 25th (I can't guarantee certain times, but I'll attempt to get to it as quickly as possible), you shall receive a PM from me containing your gift! This is the stage where you get to enjoy your gift while not knowing who made it. Post a comment on this thread with a link to your gift so others can see it. Pretty simple!

Remember to keep up the anonymity during this stage! Gifters: Don't post anything to the site, or do anything to indicate to your giftee that you made their gift. Assorted Individuals: If you know who made someone's gift, do not spoil it. Behind ghosting the exchange, this is the second worst certified Scrooge Move™, and is simply no good.

Stage 4: Lifting the Veil (Dec. 29th)

On this date, I shall make a post on this thread revealing who made who's gift! Hot chocolate and eggnog for all!

Stage 5: Optional Posting of Gifts (Anytime after Dec. 29th)

If you believe the gift you made could be posted to the site, then you are within your right to do so! Post it to the site normally, add your article to the Art Exchange Hub, and tag your article with "art-exchange". And voilà! The 2019 Art Exchange is officially over!

Become a Pinch-Hitter! (And Other Reminders)

Beyond being a regular gifter, you can also be a pinch-hitter. A pinch-hitter will make gifts for people who I haven't received gifts for from their assigned gifters. Note you'll essentially have 2 days to make however many gifts you need to make (which will, hopefully, be very little). Thus…

Only become a pinch-hitter if you're very confident in your ability. Note that I may refuse you as a pinch-hitter if I don't believe you can fulfill that role. I know it's sorta harsh, but it's necessary to ensure quality of gifts. If you'd like to be a pinch-hitter, state so at the bottom of your forum post on this thread.

You should be able to make the deadline, but if you cannot make the deadline, then I won't hold it against you. Instead, as stated above, the giftees will receive a PM stating the situation, and the pinch-hitter can either give the giftee their finished gift later, or they can give the giftee a WiP of their gift, with a promise to finish it later.

Some final (friendly) reminders:

  • Don't ghost the exchange. If you can't make a deadline, send a PM to me beforehand
  • It's Secret-Santa. Keep everything anonymous and secretive until Stage 4.
  • Let's keep it jolly! Don't make intentionally shitty gifts or gifts that don't fulfill the giftee's wishes. It's against the whole point of the exchange!

Be merry, be festive, and most importantly, have fun! Happy 8th Annual Holiday Art Exchange, everyone!

item.title: Announcement Regarding Licensing Emergency
item.content:encoded:
SCP Community,

6 months ago, we alerted you to the actions of Andrey Duksin, a Russian man who has illegally registered an illegitimate trademark for SCP within the Eurasian Customs Union. He has used said trademark to threaten and extort legitimate sellers of SCP merchandise, and in addition is guilty of copyright infringement, as his own merchandise completely violates the SCP content license: Creative Commons Share-alike 3.0. For a time, the situation calmed as we slowly pursued the dissolution of Duksin’s illegitimate trademark via Rospatent, but it has now escalated.

Duksin has recently resumed his efforts to threaten and extort competitors, and has now begun to threaten SCP itself. He utilized the illegitimate trademark to shut down the official social media page of the Russian branch of the SCP Foundation Wiki, as well as a separate fan-page. We attempted to negotiate with VK, the social media company in question, but so long as the trademark registration stands they will abide by it. Now, Duksin has followed this by making a ridiculous demand to be administrator of the Russian wiki, and that said wiki be twisted into an advertisement for his merchandise rather than the writing community that it is.

These actions threaten not only the Russian community, but every SCP branch, writer, and fan around the world. We stand with SCP-RU, reject these threats, and are organizing a lawsuit against Duksin to annul his false trademark and prevent his continued copyright infringement. As an organization of volunteers, this is a measure we do not often pursue due to the costs involved.

Last May, when news of Duksin’s actions first became public, we received many offers from generous SCP fans offering to donate to a legal fund. At the time we did not accept any offers, as we believed the situation could be resolved via bureaucracy. With these new developments, this is no longer possible. As such, we humbly ask that anyone who loves SCP and has some money to spare donate to our legal fund in order to protect our global community. We are still finalizing the details of the fundraising, and will have a second round of announcements later in the week once the donation page is ready. Donations can be made at this link.

The SCP community maintains a unified front against Duksin's threats. Please spread the word about this situation on social media using the hashtag #standwithscpru. With your help, SCP will continue to thrive.

Edit: Just a reminder that while outrage is certainly justified, please do not post any threats against anyone.

Edit: Donations can be made at this link.

item.title: Site-42 24-Hour Charity Livestream
item.description: 12:01 AM to 11:59 PM PST on Halloween. Sherm and guests read SCPs for 24 hours. Proceeds to the San Francisco LGBT Center.
item.content:encoded:
Long story short: I’m going to stream for all 24 hours of 10/31 to raise money for the SF LGBT Center. Being spooky for a good cause.

Long story long: I’ve been working on this weekly livestream show with the app GetVokl for about a month, and with Halloween coming up, I wanted to do something big for the occasion. GetVokl has agreed to partner with me to run a 24 Hour Charity Livestream. The proceeds will go to the San Francisco LGBT Center.

My plan is to mainly read SCP articles from 12:01 AM October 31st until 12:01 AM November 1st. The original broadcast will be on the GetVokl app and simulcast to the Site-42 YouTube and Twitch; because this is a charity event, those chats will be monitored as well rather than just the GetVokl chat that we do for ABS42.

Because GetVokl has the ability to have up to four people on camera at a time, I am in the process of lining up guest voices ranging from random VA and Streamer friends of mine, SCP YouTubers, SCP Authors and Wiki staff, anybody who will come by really. (I’ve also got some random theatre friends of mine in house who can take over from time to time.)

The best ways to help are really by spreading this like wildfire around the SCP community, showing up during the stream, and donating. If you’d like to do all that and volunteer for more, get in contact with me and I’ll see if I can put you to use.

This event is 100% go. I look forward to us doing a good thing this spooky season.

P.S. Thanks Zyn for moving this.

item.title: Writing Help Forum New Names
item.content:encoded:
Hi all,

Just a quick announcement that the following name changes have occurred for two subforums of the Writing Help category:

Ideas & Brainstorming —> Ideas Critique
Drafts & Critiques —> Drafts Critique

This change is to make the focus on obtaining critique clearer, as it is the expectation that material posted for reviewers to spend time helping with is well-developed and thought-out.

For authors seeking to brainstorm from less-defined ideas or desiring assistance with fleshing out a concept from general material, please use the following:

  • IRC Chatrooms: http://www.scp-wiki.net/chat-guide
  • Foundation Universe forum (for worldbuilding) : http://www.scp-wiki.net/forum/c-88827/foundation-universe
  • Questions Desk forum (for elements of the writing process): http://www.scp-wiki.net/forum/c-891087/help:questions-desk

ETA: also, big thanks to TheMightyMcB, gee0765, DrAkimoto, and Mew-ltiverse for helping with the update of the pages referencing the names!

item.title: Collaboration Contest Attribution Request Thread
item.description: Need your contest articles attributed properly? Post here!
item.content:encoded:
Due to the impending massive volume of people needing the Attribution Metadata changed, we've implemented this request thread.
Post a request with the direct names and links of your articles as well as the authors who should be credited.
Moderators/administrators will go through and update the metadata page in batches!

item.title: New Sidebar/Topbar layout!
item.content:encoded: Hey! As you can see we've made some changes to the top and sidebars. This was done to try and make more content easily accessible to the everyuser. If you notice any problems or have any feedback for us about the change, please leave it here for us. Thank you!
